2606.17053.title: Context-Aware RL for Agentic and Multimodal LLMs
2606.17053.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) often fail when answering requires identifying a small but decisive piece of evidence within a long or complex context, such as a single line in a tool trace or a subtle detail in an image. We propose ContextRL, a context-aware reinforcement learning (RL) method that improves long-horizon reasoning and multimodal performance through an \emph{indirect} auxiliary objective. Instead of supervising only the final answer, ContextRL presents the model with a query, an answer, and two highly similar contexts, and rewards it for selecting the context that supports the query--answer pair, thereby encouraging fine-grained grounding. We construct contrastive context data in two domains: for coding agents, trajectories serve as contexts, yielding 1k pairs built via condition filtering; for multimodal reasoning, images serve as contexts, yielding 7K pairs built via generative editing and similarity search. ContextRL achieves average gains of +2.2% over standard GRPO on 5 long-horizon benchmarks, and +1.8% across 12 diverse visual question answering benchmarks. To disentangle the effect of the proposed objective from that of additional data, we compare against data-augmentation baselines that repurpose the same contrastive contexts as standard query--context--answer examples. These baselines provide little to no improvement, showing that the gains arise from the proposed context-selection objective rather than from the contrastive data alone.
2606.17053.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在回答需要在長或複雜的上下文中識別一小段但關鍵證據時，經常會失敗，例如工具追蹤中的單行或圖像中的微妙細節。我們提出了ContextRL，一種上下文感知的強化學習（RL）方法，通過\emph{間接}輔助目標來改善長期推理和多模態性能。ContextRL不僅僅監督最終答案，而是向模型提供一個查詢、一個答案和兩個高度相似的上下文，並對選擇支持查詢-答案對的上下文進行獎勵，從而鼓勵細緻的基礎。 我們在兩個領域構建對比上下文數據：對於編碼代理，軌跡作為上下文，通過條件過濾生成1k對；對於多模態推理，圖像作為上下文，通過生成編輯和相似性搜索生成7K對。ContextRL在5個長期基準上相對於標準GRPO實現了平均+2.2%的增益，在12個多樣的視覺問題回答基準上實現了+1.8%的增益。為了區分所提議目標的效果與額外數據的效果，我們與數據增強基準進行比較，這些基準重新利用相同的對比上下文作為標準查詢-上下文-答案示例。這些基準幾乎沒有改善，顯示出增益來自於所提出的上下文選擇目標，而非僅僅來自對比數據。
2606.17053.author: Peiyang Xu et.al.
2606.17053.authors: Peiyang Xu, Bangzheng Li, Sijia Liu, Karthik R. Narasimhan, Pramod Viswanath, Prateek Mittal, Xingyu Fu
